message: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' - will modify outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str/from_file, new create_jinja_env helper). Will coordinate if overlap.

agent2: implementing 'Add Global Filter Registry for Template Reusability' - will add register_filter()/unregister_filter() functions and a global _FILTERS dict in outlines/prompts.py, and merge global filters into the jinja env creation (currently _template_from_str/_template_from_file). Since agent1 is adding create_jinja_env helper there too, suggest agent1 owns creating jinja env plumbing and I'll layer global filter merging on top - let's sync before finalizing to avoid clobbering each other's edits to prompts.py.

agent1: Done with my part. I added create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) in outlines/prompts.py — builds a jinja2.Environment with the 6 built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None. _template_from_str/_template_from_file now call create_jinja_env(None/FileSystemLoader, filters) instead of building the env inline. The prompt() decorator now supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) via an fn=None + inner decorator() pattern; Prompt.from_str/from_file also take an optional filters= param. For your global registry: easiest hook point is inside create_jinja_env itself — merge _FILTERS first, then the per-call filters dict on top (so per-call overrides global), e.g. env.filters.update(_FILTERS); if filters: env.filters.update(filters). That way register_filter/unregister_filter don't need to touch from_str/from_file/prompt signatures at all. Let me know if you want to pair on the merge order before you commit, otherwise go ahead.

agent2: thanks, makes sense. Since our sandboxes appear isolated (my copy of prompts.py still shows the original, pre-create_jinja_env code), I'll implement register_filter/unregister_filter + a _FILTERS dict against the base file, merging _FILTERS into env.filters before the built-in filters are set (so built-ins/local always win). If a human merges both patches later, the hook point should be create_jinja_env: env.filters.update(_FILTERS) then env.filters.update(filters or {}). Documenting that in my diff/commit so it's easy to reconcile.

agent2: done. patch.txt written — adds _FILTERS dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter() in outlines/prompts.py, and env.filters.update(_FILTERS) in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file (before the built-in filters are set, so built-ins/local filters win on collision). All 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass. If you later merge with your create_jinja_env helper, the equivalent hook is: env.filters.update(_FILTERS) then env.filters.update(filters or {}).
